data.title: Heiwado Posts Modest Sales Growth but Lower Profit, Lifts Dividend and Targets Earnings Recovery
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

HEIWADO CO ( (JP:8276) ) has issued an announcement.

Heiwado Co., Ltd., a Japanese retailer listed on the Tokyo Stock Exchange, reported modest top-line growth for the fiscal year ended February 20, 2026, with operating revenue rising 2.5% to ¥456.0 billion. Despite this, operating and ordinary profit were essentially flat, and profit attributable to owners of the parent declined 12.3% to ¥9.4 billion, leading to a drop in basic earnings per share to ¥189.29.

The company strengthened its financial position, with total assets increasing to ¥313.1 billion and the equity ratio improving to 62.5%, while cash and cash equivalents at period-end fell to ¥19.2 billion as operating cash flow decreased and investment outlays rose. Heiwado raised its annual dividend from ¥63.00 to ¥66.00 per share, signaling continued shareholder returns, and issued forecasts for fiscal 2027 that project 4.8% growth in operating revenue and a 4.1% increase in profit attributable to owners of the parent, suggesting expectations of a gradual earnings recovery despite recent profit pressure.

More about HEIWADO CO

Heiwado Co., Ltd. is a Japanese retail company listed on the Tokyo Stock Exchange, operating primarily in the general merchandise and supermarket sector. The group focuses on regional retailing, generating operating revenue from store-based sales and related services, with operations concentrated in its domestic market under Japanese GAAP reporting standards.

Average Trading Volume: 173,139

Current Market Cap: Yen134.8B

Vicinity Centres ( (AU:VCX) ) has issued an update.

Vicinity Centres has reported the cessation of a total of 306,377 equity-linked securities after certain performance and restricted rights lapsed at the end of March 2026. The affected instruments comprised 260,836 performance rights and 45,541 restricted rights that expired because their vesting conditions were not met or became incapable of being satisfied, marginally reducing potential future share dilution and signalling that specific performance hurdles or service conditions tied to these awards were not achieved.

data.title: Cerrado Gold Posts Record 2025 EBITDA as Minera Don Nicolas Ramps Up Underground Output
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest update is out from Cerrado Gold ( (TSE:CERT) ).

Cerrado Gold reported 2025 annual production of 50,238 gold equivalent ounces at its Minera Don Nicolas mine, delivering adjusted EBITDA of US$46.1 million and maintaining all-in sustaining costs in line with guidance despite inflation and water-related constraints. The company ended the year with US$22.1 million in cash and expects 2026 output of 50,000 to 60,000 gold equivalent ounces, underpinned by a ramp-up in underground mining, operational optimizations, and continued exploration that could materially expand its low-cost production profile.

Management highlighted that record quarterly and annual adjusted EBITDA was driven by stable costs and stronger gold prices, while a completed hedging program leaves the company fully exposed to future upside in bullion markets. With ongoing work on a bankable feasibility study at Mont Sorcier and advancing the Lagoa Salgada project, Cerrado is leveraging cash flow from Minera Don Nicolas to strengthen its financial position and potentially enhance long-term growth across its portfolio.

The most recent analyst rating on (TSE:CERT) stock is a Buy with a C$3.30 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Cerrado Gold stock, see the TSE:CERT Stock Forecast page.

More about Cerrado Gold

Cerrado Gold Inc. is a precious and base metals company focused on gold and iron ore assets across the Americas and Europe. Its key operations include the Minera Don Nicolas gold project in Argentina, the Lagoa Salgada VMS project in Portugal, and the Mont Sorcier high-purity DRI iron project in Quebec, positioning the company as a diversified metals producer with both production and development-stage assets.

Imagion Biosystems Ltd. ( (AU:IBX) ) has issued an announcement.

Imagion Biosystems has reported a change to its capital structure following the cessation of a tranche of its convertible securities. The company notified the ASX that 300,000 IBXAAQ convertible notes have been repaid or redeemed without conversion, reflecting a reduction in its outstanding convertible debt as of January 8, 2026.

The repayment or redemption of these notes signals a shift in the company’s financing profile, potentially lowering future dilution risk for existing shareholders. This adjustment may also improve balance-sheet clarity by simplifying the mix of debt and equity-linked instruments on issue, which could be relevant for investors assessing Imagion’s capital management strategy.

The latest update is out from China Sanjiang Fine Chemicals Co., Ltd. ( (HK:2198) ).

China Sanjiang Fine Chemicals Company Limited has updated its annual results announcement for the year ended 31 December 2025 to revise details of its final cash dividend. The board now proposes a final ordinary dividend of HKD 0.05 per share, payable in Hong Kong dollars following shareholder approval on 22 May 2026.

The ex-dividend date is set for 28 May 2026, with the record date on 2 June 2026 and payment scheduled for 23 June 2026. The update provides shareholders and potential investors with a clear timetable for entitlement and distribution, underscoring the company’s continued practice of returning cash to shareholders in line with its disclosed results for 2025.

data.description:

  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

JiaHua Stores Holdings Limited ( (HK:0602) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Jiahua Stores Holdings Limited has confirmed the current composition of its board of directors, led by chairman and executive director Zhuang Lu Kun alongside fellow executive directors Zhuang Pei Zhong and Zhuang Xiao Xiong, and supported by four independent non-executive directors. The company has also detailed the membership and chairmanship of its three key board committees for audit, remuneration and nomination matters, a move that clarifies governance responsibilities for investors and underscores its adherence to Hong Kong listing and corporate governance standards.

The audit committee is chaired by independent non-executive director Chin Kam Cheung, with independent directors Sun Ju Yi, Ai Ji and Madam Ying Chi Kwan serving as members. The remuneration committee is chaired by Sun Ju Yi and the nomination committee by Ai Ji, with overlapping independent director participation across all three bodies, signaling an emphasis on independent oversight of financial reporting, executive pay and board appointments.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:0602) stock is a Hold with a HK$0.05 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on JiaHua Stores Holdings Limited stock, see the HK:0602 Stock Forecast page.

Jiahua Stores Holdings Limited is a Hong Kong–listed company incorporated in the Cayman Islands and engaged in the retail sector, operating department store-style outlets under the Jiahua brand. data.title: Image Resources Sets AGM Date as It Advances Growth and Value-Add Strategy
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Image Resources NL ( (AU:IMA) ) has shared an announcement.

Image Resources NL has scheduled its annual general meeting for 28 May 2026, with the re-election of directors on the agenda and a 9 April 2026 deadline for receipt of director nominations at its registered office. The meeting comes as the company continues successful operations at its Atlas project and advances its broader strategy to expand production, diversify products and enhance value through a lower-emissions, provisionally patented process to upgrade ilmenite to synthetic rutile, reinforcing its position in the global critical minerals market.

Image’s transition from a single-mine, single-product supplier into a multi-mine, multi-product producer selling into global markets underscores its ambition to grow scale and sustainability in mineral sands. The company’s focus on innovation, including demonstrating the technical and economic feasibility of its synthetic rutile process in 2026, has potential implications for operating efficiency, environmental footprint and long-term competitiveness for investors and customers alike.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:IMA) stock is a Hold with a A$0.05 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Image Resources NL stock, see the AU:IMA Stock Forecast page.

More about Image Resources NL

Image Resources NL is an Australian mineral sands miner focused on supplying critical minerals such as titanium dioxide, zircon and monazite containing rare earth elements to global markets. The company has a track record of on-time, on-budget project delivery, including the Boonanarring mine and the Atlas project in Western Australia, and is pursuing a growth strategy involving multiple mines, diversified products and broader international customer reach.

data.title: Taiyo Holdings Backs KKR-Led Tender Offer to Go Private and Accelerate Growth
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest update is out from Taiyo Holdings Co., Ltd. ( (JP:4626) ).

Taiyo Holdings has agreed that KJ005 Co., Ltd., owned by investment funds managed by KKR, will launch a tender offer to take the company private, a move its board supports as of March 31, 2026. Management expects that private ownership under KKR will secure a more stable shareholder base, enable faster, long-term oriented decision-making, and leverage KKR’s global network and sector expertise to accelerate growth and structural reforms across its semiconductor and medical-related businesses.

Under KKR’s ownership, Taiyo plans to continue executing its long-term management plan “Beyond Imagination 2030” while pursuing additional “Beyond Boundaries” initiatives that it believes would be difficult to realize alone. The company views the transaction as expanding growth opportunities and enhancing medium- to long-term corporate value, asserting that it aligns with the common interests of shareholders by supporting sustained strategic execution rather than short-term market pressures.

The most recent analyst rating on (JP:4626) stock is a Buy with a Yen6005.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Taiyo Holdings Co., Ltd. stock, see the JP:4626 Stock Forecast page.

More about Taiyo Holdings Co., Ltd.

Taiyo Holdings Co., Ltd., listed on the Tokyo Stock Exchange Prime Market under code 4626, operates in businesses tied to the semiconductor and medical and pharmaceutical fields. Through its portfolio, the company focuses on growth in technology-driven markets, positioning itself to capitalize on rising demand in advanced electronics and healthcare-related sectors.

data.title: Regener8 Resources Sets May 2026 General Meeting, Moves to Digital Notices
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Regener8 Resources NL ( (AU:R8R) ) has issued an announcement.

Regener8 Resources NL has called a general meeting of shareholders for 6 May 2026 in West Perth, setting 4 May 2026 as the record date to determine voting eligibility. The company will hold the meeting in person, with all resolutions decided by poll, and is encouraging shareholders to vote in advance or appoint the chair as proxy.

In line with recent changes to the Corporations Act, Regener8 will not mail hard copies of the notice of meeting unless shareholders have specifically elected to receive them, instead providing electronic access via the ASX website. The notice also outlines proxy voting rules and stresses the importance of timely submission of proxy forms to ensure votes are counted, underscoring the company’s focus on regulatory compliance and shareholder participation.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:R8R) stock is a Hold with a A$0.19 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Regener8 Resources NL stock, see the AU:R8R Stock Forecast page.

More about Regener8 Resources NL

Regener8 Resources NL is an Australian-listed company in the resources sector, with a shareholder base governed under the Corporations Act 2001 (Cth). The company’s securities trade on the ASX, and it conducts its corporate governance and shareholder communications in line with Australian regulatory requirements for listed resource companies.
